things.name: Rennes Parliament of Brittany
things.description: Magnificent seventeenth-century palace that was the parliament of independent Brittany, with richly decorated gilded chambers

things.name: Place des Lices
things.description: Historic square hosting Brittany's largest Saturday market since the Middle Ages, overflowing with local produce and crafts

things.name: Vieux Rennes (Old Town)
things.description: Enchanting medieval quarter of half-timbered houses that miraculously survived the great fire of 1720, now a lively dining hub

things.name: Musée de Bretagne
things.description: Comprehensive museum exploring Brittany's unique Celtic heritage, from prehistoric megaliths to contemporary culture

things.name: Les Champs Libres
things.description: Striking contemporary cultural complex housing the museum, science gallery and regional library in one landmark building

things.name: Cathédrale Saint-Pierre
things.description: Neoclassical cathedral completed in 1844 after 57 years of construction, with a magnificent gilded altarpiece inside

things.name: Parc du Thabor
things.description: Beautifully maintained French formal garden and English park with a rose garden of over 1800 varieties in the city centre

things.name: Musée des Beaux-Arts de Rennes
things.description: Fine arts museum with 5000 works spanning antiquity to the present, including a rare Da Vinci drawing and important Breton school paintings

things.name: Place de la Mairie
things.description: Elegant central square flanked by the neoclassical town hall and opera house, the formal heart of 18th-century Rennes

things.name: Écomusée du Pays de Rennes
things.description: Living farm museum on a historic country estate showing Breton rural life across the centuries with rare breed animals
